inspected_files.finding: Defines CurrentUserContext from verified DB-user JWT, active user roles, primary role/tenant, memberships and objectScopes.
Exposes current-user context and fails closed with 401, no mutation, no advice execution and no client release.
Defines canonical auth cookie alphavest_auth_jwt, issuer/provider db-user-jwt, expiry and verification.
Maps dummy provider session output into safe DB-user JWT claims.
DB-backed dummy auth provider loads user, role assignment and tenant, writes auth audit events and issues demo session token/JWT inputs.
Legacy/demo helper creates role/tenant sessions from static demo actors and includes permissive createDemoSession fallbacks plus stricter tryCreateDemoSession/requireDemoSession.
Current proof target for MFA-to-current-user resolution, fail-closed current-user 401 and auth bridge behavior.
Current proof target for strict demo-session mapping and fallback-risk documentation.

canonical_session_contract.canonical_source: DB-user JWT resolved by resolveCurrentUserFromRequest and /api/current-user.

allowed_use: Playwright/API tests that do not claim production authentication
Legacy service helpers until migrated behind explicit adapters
Route smoke and visual capture setup where the claim is UI availability, not permission truth

legacy_debt_to_remove.debt: createDemoSession silently falls back to Bennett/compliance when role or tenant is invalid.
legacy_debt_to_remove.risk: Invalid or stale test inputs can look valid and mask access-control bugs.
legacy_debt_to_remove.bold_recommendation: Move new protected services to requireDemoSession or CurrentUserContext, and progressively ban createDemoSession in safety-critical tests.

legacy_debt_to_remove.debt: Several APIs still accept roleKey/tenantSlug query parameters as actor context.
legacy_debt_to_remove.risk: Actor identity can be confused with requested object scope.
legacy_debt_to_remove.bold_recommendation: For P0 protected APIs, require DB-user JWT current-user context; keep query parameters only for read-only demo catalog surfaces or explicit fixture adapters.

legacy_debt_to_remove.debt: Dummy auth session cookie and DB-user JWT coexist.
legacy_debt_to_remove.risk: Tooling may assume dummy cookie is equivalent to current-user JWT.
legacy_debt_to_remove.bold_recommendation: Treat alphavest_dummy_auth_session as app-route render access only; DB/API process work must use alphavest_auth_jwt.

short_term: Keep DB-user JWT current-user as canonical for journey APIs and any P0 process mutation.
Keep demo-session helpers for tests, but prefer requireDemoSession over createDemoSession when asserting security behavior.
Mark every query-param actor API as demo/readmodel or migration-needed, not canonical P0 authority.
medium_term: Introduce a single CurrentUserContext-to-DemoSession adapter only where legacy services still require DemoSession.
Remove silent fallbacks from safety-critical tests.
Make objectScopes first-class in permission and client visibility proof.
non_goals_for_bd_02_a: Do not remove createDemoSession in this subtask.
Do not change current-user response shape without BD-02-B/BD-02-C proof.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Legacy debt is not disguised as acceptable architecture.

overclaim_guard: BD-02-A locks the session context contract only. It does not prove role-to-object-scope permissions or negative access behavior; those are BD-02-B and BD-02-C.
